exemplar.title: How is the Theme of Prejudice in Red Scarf Girl by Ji-Li Jiang and The Kite Runner by Khaled Hosseini treated?

detailedExemplarData.title: How is the Theme of Prejudice in Red Scarf Girl by Ji-Li Jiang and The Kite Runner by Khaled Hosseini treated?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately with clear purpose and focus throughout the essay
Research question is clearly stated, focused and consistently linked to the discussion
weaknesses: Methodology is narrowly confined to close reading of primary texts with no explicit description of method or engagement with secondary sources
Occasional verbosity and minor lapses in signposting between sections dilute concision and precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic clarity and research question) are at the upper band, but the methodology strand is constrained by a limited range of sources and lack of an explicit methods section. On balance, the work sits solidly in the mid-level of the 3â4 band, meriting 4 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates sound knowledge of both primary texts with accurate contextual background and well-chosen quotations
Use of literary terminology is generally accurate and aids analysis
weaknesses: No engagement with secondary scholarship limits the breadth and depth of understanding
Occasional imprecision in terminology (e.g., earlier misspelling of âsimileâ) indicates inconsistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Knowledge of the texts is good but unaugmented by wider sources, placing the work in the lower half of the 3â4 band. Terminology is mostly accurate but not sufficiently consistent to justify a higher score.

strengths: Analysis moves beyond summary to interpret key literary techniques and supports claims with apt quotations
Argument is coherent and comparative, with a clear line of reasoning throughout
weaknesses: Research is confined to primary texts, with no engagement with critical or theoretical perspectives
Sections occasionally drift into plot summary and the evaluation remains surface-level without deeper interrogation of implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates âgoodâ analysis and argument structure but is hampered by minimal research scope and repetitive summary. These factors place it at the bottom of the 4â6 band, justifying 4 out of 12.

strengths: Includes all required structural components (title page, contents, headings, conclusion, references)
Consistent citation style and page numbering support readability
weaknesses: Misalignment between the table of contents and actual page numbers
Inconsistent spacing and incomplete publication details in the reference list detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the organisation and basic formatting meet requirements, persistent minor layout and referencing lapses place the work in the lower half of the competent band, meriting 2 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodology relies almost exclusively on close reading of the two primary texts; there is no meaningful engagement with secondary or theoretical sources beyond a brief definition of prejudice. While the essay is organised into clear thematic lenses (symbolism, language, perspective), the absence of a broader critical framework or scholarly dialogue limits evidence of informed source selection and range. Thus, the methodology is present but remains limited and only partially fit for purpose.

Pages are numbered, the title and word count are provided, and a consistent citation style is generally applied. Minor inconsistencies appear in the reference list (e.g., incomplete publication details) and there are occasional formatting lapses in headings and spacing, yet overall layout conventions are followed well enough to facilitate readability. These small issues prevent achievement of full marks but satisfy the criterion at the competent level.

Research underpinning the essay is confined to the two primary texts, with no substantive engagement with literary criticism, historical scholarship, or theoretical perspectives that could enrich or challenge the argument. While the chosen textual evidence is pertinent, the limited scope of sources constrains the depth and breadth of enquiry, placing the work in the band where research is present but of minimal range and only partly relevant to the question.

The student introduces the topicâthe literary treatment of prejudice in two specified textsâfrom the outset and sustains this focus throughout the essay. The introduction supplies clear contextual background on each work and explicitly frames prejudice as the central organising theme, signalling a coherent analytical purpose. Occasional verbosity slightly dilutes concision, yet the topicâs scope and direction remain evident and appropriate to the subject.

The student constructs a clear line of argument contrasting how each author portrays prejudice and concludes with a reflective synthesis. There is evidence of evaluation (e.g., discussion of emotional vs. societal portrayals, consideration of authorial goals). However, evaluation tends to remain surface-level, with limited interrogation of implications or alternative readings, and the conclusion reiterates points rather than offering a fully critical reflection. This situates the work in the adequate-to-good range.

The analysis moves beyond description to interpret literary techniques (e.g., symbolism of the red scarf and kite, diction revealing dehumanisation, narrative perspective). Claims are generally supported with apt quotations, and the comparative framework is coherent. Nevertheless, several sections drift into summary, and some assertions about authorial intention or social impact lack substantiation from wider research. Consequently, the depth and consistency of analysis align with the lower end of the âgoodâ band.

The essay includes all required structural components: title page, abstract-style introduction, table of contents, clearly headed sections, conclusion, and references, meeting the formal expectations of the EE. Some misalignment between the table of contents and page numbering, as well as uneven paragraphing, detract from overall polish, but the structure remains logically organised and supports the readerâs navigation.

The student demonstrates sound knowledge of both primary texts, summarising historical contexts and relevant plot events accurately and integrating well-chosen quotations. However, knowledge is drawn almost wholly from the literary works themselves; secondary scholarship is absent, restricting the breadth of understanding and leaving some interpretive claims unexplored. Application of knowledge is therefore good but not consistently thorough or supported by wider sources.

Literary terminology (e.g., symbolism, diction, juxtaposition, foreshadowing, irony, dehumanisation) is generally accurate and integrated naturally into the analysis, showing solid conceptual grasp. There are occasional inaccuracies or imprecisions (e.g., mis-spelling "simile" earlier as âsimilarâ), yet these lapses are infrequent and do not undermine overall clarity. Usage is mostly consistent and aids the readerâs understanding of the analytical points.

The research questionââHow is the theme of prejudice â¦ treated?ââis explicitly stated, comparative, and suitably narrow for a 4 000-word EE. Each major section (symbolism, language, perspective) is clearly linked back to this guiding question, demonstrating that the investigation is structured around it. Minor lapses in signposting between sections prevent a perfect score, but the overall connection between RQ and discussion is strong and sustained.

annotations.comment: This paragraph opens with broad plot summary; focus more narrowly on how narrative choices reveal systemic prejudice.
The citation for Jiangâs memoir is prominently included; ensure consistency by adding full publisher details.
The symbolism analysis draws only on the primary texts; integrating secondary literary criticism would evidence deeper research and support.
The evolving interpretation of the red scarf symbol is insightful and well supported with pertinent textual evidence.
Several sentences recount plot details at length; condense summary to maintain analytic focus on how the symbol conveys prejudice.
The emotional connotations of 'tension' and 'anxiety' are accurately linked to prejudiceâs psychological impact in Red Scarf Girl.
The shift into the 'Perspective' section is abrupt; adding a transitional sentence would improve coherence and reader guidance.
The metaphor of soap bubbles as foreshadowing effectively conveys the fragility of Ji-Liâs aspirations under systemic prejudice.
Some page ranges in the Table of Contents do not align precisely with the documentâs pagination; update these to ensure accurate signposting.
The contextual background on both texts demonstrates sound knowledge of historical and thematic frameworks.
The opening to the critical discussion spends substantial space on summary rather than engaging immediately with the research question; consider tightening the focus.
The introduction to the Symbolism section is largely descriptive; refine this transition to articulate the analytical purpose more clearly.
Typographical error: 'similar' is used instead of 'simile' when discussing the tumor metaphor; precise terminology is essential.
Analysis of dehumanizing language is descriptive; consider incorporating scholarly perspectives on propaganda and dehumanization for depth.
The examination of Amirâs internalized prejudice adds valuable complexity to the discussion of bias and power dynamics.
This section relies heavily on dialogue summary; replace portions with analysis of how specific language choices perpetuate systemic prejudice.
The discussion of first-person narration insightfully shows how perspective shapes reader understanding of prejudice.
The conclusion reiterates comparative points but lacks synthesis of broader implications; consider integrating evaluative insight to close the argument.
The final rhetorical question raises important issues but is not tied back to specific analytical findings; link it explicitly to your conclusions.
There is no dedicated reflection on your research process or decision-making; include an evaluative reflection to meet the engagement criterion.
The Table of Contents is included and clearly formatted, facilitating navigation through the extended essay structure.
There is no explicit methodology section or description of secondary sources; outlining methodological choices would strengthen evidence of informed selection.
The definition of prejudice is precise and frames the thematic analysis effectively, showing strong conceptual grasp.
The kiteâs symbolism is contextualized through emotional stakes in a key scene, illustrating strong comparative insight.
The bibliography omits secondary sources and has inconsistent publication details, limiting the scope and credibility of your research.
The inclusion and clear formatting of the investigationâs title at the beginning complies with IB requirements and immediately establishes the reportâs focus.
The word count is provided at the start, demonstrating adherence to the 4 000-word limit requirement.
The research question is stated clearly and comparatively focused, effectively framing the investigationâs scope.
